name: Quick-Fire_Mech

infobox.Systems: Quick-Fire Rockets(Brute)EffectFire two projectiles in different directions.(1 damage)Upgrade IAdd Push ()Will now push both targets.Upgrade II+1 Damage ()+1 damage to both targets.

sections.Team: This mech is available as part of theHeat Sinkerssquad.
sections.Strategy:
The Quick-Fire Mech is comparable to theMirror Mechin that it's forced to shoot two projectiles, with the key advantage of being able to choose which direction the second projectile is fired. Unlike the Mirror Mech however, Quick-Fire Rockets does not have starting push and must be powered as an upgrade. The early-game can be challenging as a result of its low damage output and lack of displacement.
Add Pushis a vital first upgrade, as it gives the Mech a way to reposition enemies and get bump damage. In the Heat Sinkers, consider a pilot that boosts the Quick-Fire Mech (Kai Miller,Morgan Lejeune) so that it is not as reliant on consuming fire tiles from its partners. Upgrading Quick-Fire's+1 Damageto each target is expensive at 3 cores, but it should be considered eventually if doing a three or four island run. The Quick-Fire Mech is also rather frail at the start of the timeline, so boosting its health eventually is recommended;Mafanis another viable pilot choice to start, as he comes with an extra Reactor Core thanks to his ability to immediately allow for push, while boosting the survivability thanks to Zoltan Shield.
